meta.title: Why Fibe?
meta.description: Whether you've never written code, run a research lab, or ship startups in your sleep — Fibe is the missing workshop. Pick your path.
meta.keywords: Fibe, fibe.gg, no-code, low-code, AI agent, Docker environments, founder, CTO, AI research, Genie, Marquee, Playground, dev environment, vibe coding
meta.og_title: Why Fibe — six paths into the same workshop
meta.og_description: No experience. Deep expertise. Researcher. Founder. CTO. Fibe meets each of you where you are.
meta.twitter_title: Why Fibe — six paths into the same workshop
meta.twitter_description: No experience. Deep expertise. Researcher. Founder. CTO. Fibe meets each of you where you are.

hero.eyebrow: Why you might really want to try Fibe?
hero.title:
Everyone needs a smartphone
Many doubted in 2007
hero.lede:
Today we all depend on software.
            Internet created apps which smartphones brought to our pockets.
            Fibe tailors them precisely to what we need

graph.aria: Many alternative paths converging into a single shortest path — the Fibe Way

matrix.eyebrow: What could will you do with Fibe?
matrix.title: Many reasons to use a smartphone
matrix.sub: Even more to use Fibe. Reasons depend on your background

fun.title: For fun
fun.sub: Custom game, ad-hoc animation, throwaway experiment — on the go.

problem.title: Solve a problem
problem.sub: Landing, CRM, internal tool — without hiring an agency.

sharpen.title: Sharpen your tools
sharpen.sub: Work from mobile, create unique tools, automate everything — during a daily.

product.title: Funnel the expertise
product.sub: Build that custom integration, make this validation fire — without becoming a founder (or?..)

compete.title: Compete with Them
compete.sub: Fight network effect and unlimited resources with precision strikes and full focus — during the weekends.

cto.title: AI-native on the ground, not on paper
cto.sub: QA prepares a playground with a bug-fix. Products transform user stories to environments and PRs. Sentry issues become a URL with a fix — thats could become a reality, not just a pitch for a next round.

research.title: Visit The Edge
research.sub: Provide agents with real environments in a perfect sandbox: granular permissions, tailored security, YOLO — assemble, not reinvent.

fun.title:
Software,
for fun
fun.lede_0:
You want a Discord bot or a Teamspeak server for your gaming group,
              a custom Alias with your own rules,
              a multi-player tower-defense game
              or a birthday postcard with a bad joke for all to see?
fun.lede_1: Just let Fibe do it all for you:
fun.list_0: Simple chat — treat it like an always-on IT guy just for you.
fun.list_1: No hassle with servers or domains — you're just having fun.
fun.list_2: Bring your own ideas — or start from someone else's hack.
fun.action_sub: No revenue model required. Just curiosity and an empty Saturday.

slide_0.title: A Discord bot for the gaming group
slide_0.sub: Stand it up Saturday, leave it running on a tiny host.

slide_1.title: Learn a new framework, by using it
slide_1.sub: Clean room, throwaway env. Break it, fix it, try again.

slide_2.sub: Custom URL, ten minutes, no DevOps required.

slide_3.title: Spin up, destroy, no regrets
slide_3.sub: When the experiment ends, the bill ends.

problem.chip_tag: Software is not a problem to solve, anymore
problem.title:
Embrace software —
assist your business
problem.lede_0:
You want a landing page for your bakery, a CRM your customers can actually book through,
              an inventory tracker for the warehouse, an internal tool that ends the Slack ping-pong —
              and an agency just quoted you six figures?
problem.list_0: Pre-built recipes — landing pages, CRMs, portfolios, simple shops.
problem.list_1: No-code, UI-driven — achieve your goal without becoming an IT expert.
problem.list_3: AI assistant — always ready to help as soon as you want to dive deeper.
problem.action_sub: Each cut is its own video on Rumble — pick the one that smells most like yours.

slide_0.title: Landing page in an afternoon
slide_0.sub: Anya rebuilds her bakery's site on a Saturday.

slide_1.title: Your first real CRM
slide_1.sub: Replacing a 40-tab spreadsheet without writing code.

slide_2.title: An internal tool your team will use
slide_2.sub: A scheduling board that ends the Slack ping-pong.

slide_3.title: Portfolio that actually ships
slide_3.sub: From Dribbble pile to live URL in one session.

product.title:
Your experience,
shaped as workflows
product.lede_0: You know what dentists, truckers, architects, farmers actually need? Or you're an illustrator, an animator, a sound designer — and the engineering around your craft has always eaten your night?
product.lede_1: You handle features, Fibe takes care of details:
product.list_0: Talk to an AI assistant — plain language about your craft, your industry, your niche.
product.list_1: See it run at a URL — your first customer, your first viewer.
product.list_2: Iterate without fear — branch a copy, try a render, throw it away, try again.
product.action_sub: Real domains, real builders shipping with Fibe + their first AI assistant.

slide_0.title: Dental scheduling, end to end
slide_0.sub: Practice owner + AI assistant ship a real product.

slide_1.title: Dispatch board for truckers
slide_1.sub: A coordinator's clipboard becomes a real app.

slide_2.title: Architect ↔ client portal
slide_2.sub: Drawings, approvals, and invoicing in one place.

slide_4.title: From sketch to render farm
slide_4.sub: An illustrator turns her style guide into a full video pipeline.

slide_5.title: A playable build, sent as a URL
slide_5.sub: Solo game dev drops the 4 GB ZIP for a link.

sharpen.chip_tag: Finish what you had never time for
sharpen.title:
Perfect setup,
just for you,
during a daily
sharpen.lede_0: You can ship anything — except the things you actually wanted to ship for yourself. The e2e suite still on the TODO list. The self-host you keep almost-finishing. The pet project waiting on a free Saturday. Always fifteen minutes between meetings, never a clear afternoon?
sharpen.list_0: Finally finish that CI — the e2e suite, the deploy pipeline, the integration tests you always meant to wire up but never had a clear afternoon for.
sharpen.list_1: Self-host your own way — your own machine, your own GPU, your own rules. The exact environment you would set up, set up once and ready everywhere.
sharpen.list_2: Ship from your phone — pet projects, side bets, weekend ideas. From the train, the couch, or during a daily.
sharpen.action_sub: Short walkthroughs of the moves that pay back the most context-switching minutes.

slide_1.title: Test suites in clean rooms
slide_1.sub: Tests blow up safely so prod never does.

slide_2.title: Three branches, three environments
slide_2.sub: Compare feature bets side by side.

slide_3.title: Your editor, your AI, our environments
slide_3.sub: Drive it with whatever agent you already use.

compete.eyebrow: How to compete with Them using Fibe?
compete.title:
Outship
the incumbents.
compete.lede_0: They have a hundred engineers, unlimited compute, a marketing budget the size of your runway — and no focus, no dedication, no vision anyone can actually point at. You have all three?
compete.lede_1: Just turn that asymmetry into a shipped product:
compete.list_0: You know what to build — years of thinking about it, days from shipping. They're still routing the proposal through three layers of approval.
compete.list_1: You know how to build it — the architecture, the trade-offs, the right calls already made in your head. No design committees, no rewrites.
compete.list_2: Brain beats headcount — engineering experience plus domain expertise, in one head, with Fibe handling the boring parts. The combination that's never had this kind of leverage before.
compete.action_sub: Operators with leverage you didn't have last year — how they actually run a week.

slide_0.title: Ten product bets, one operator
slide_0.sub: Parallel environments — and where to kill them.

slide_1.title: An AI swarm shipping features
slide_1.sub: Specialists per job, one ship date.

slide_3.title: Fork a clean stack → head start
slide_3.sub: Skip the first 20% — pull a recipe someone else already debugged.

cto.eyebrow: How to be AI-native on the ground, not on paper?
cto.title:
Stop the AI theatre.
Start the shipping.
cto.lede_0: The board demands AI-native. Investors demand AI-native. Your competitors are tweeting AI-native. And not one of them — not the consultants, not the analysts, not the LinkedIn keynotes — can tell you what AI-native actually means. The quarterly review is in six weeks. The strategy memo is overdue?
cto.lede_1: Just make it real on the ground:
cto.list_0: Show, don't tell — engineers spin up a playground for the bug-fix and post the link in Slack. Product turns a user story into a working PR overnight. Sentry issues come back as URLs with the fix already attached. Each one is a release note, not another roadmap promise.
cto.list_1: Stop renting your future — own the chat, the docs, the identity, the calls. Self-host on your hardware. The per-seat SaaS bill stops being a tax on every new hire. Your CFO finally has good news in the line item they've been avoiding for years.
cto.list_2: Walk in different — into the next board meeting, the next press cycle, the next investor call. Not with another transformation deck. With a live URL of what your team actually shipped this quarter. The buzzword stops being a threat — it becomes your demo.
cto.action_sub: QA, Product, and Eng moving in parallel — without rebuilding your platform.

slide_0.title: QA on a fresh staging copy
slide_0.sub: Click → environment → bug found by lunch.

slide_2.title: Audit log, vault, 2FA
slide_2.sub: The boring done right, for compliance week.

slide_3.title: Bring your own Docker hosts
slide_3.sub: Environments on your fleet, your network, your rules.

slide_4.title: Team chat on your hardware
slide_4.sub: Replace a per-seat SaaS tax with a few cents of compute.

slide_5.title: Single sign-on, no per-seat ransom
slide_5.sub: One login for every tool, owned by you.

slide_6.title: Wiki & docs inside your walls
slide_6.sub: The team brain — not in someone else's cloud.

research.eyebrow: How to visit The Edge safely?
research.title:
Real systems.
Hard isolation.
research.lede_0:
Evaluating an agent on real software stops being safe the moment you let it touch
              your own machine, your VPN, your secrets, your shared dev cluster?
research.lede_1: Just hand it The Edge — and nothing more:
research.list_0: Hard isolation, granular control — one environment per experiment, with permissions, network egress, mounts, secrets and resource caps you set per run. Hand the agent The Edge — and nothing more.
research.list_1: Configure every agent per-experiment — skills, prompt, arguments, mounted files, tools allowed and tools blocked. Pin it in the recipe, reproduce it bit-for-bit, diff two runs in seconds.
research.list_2: Swap LLM providers like batteries — local GPU, hosted, frontier, a private endpoint, last week's model versus this week's. Same experiment, same harness, different brain — compare with a single env variable.
research.action_sub: Real evaluations and benchmarks running on Fibe — every artifact captured.

slide_0.title: Evaluating an agent on a real repo
slide_0.sub: One environment, one agent, one verdict.

slide_3.title: Capturing every artifact
slide_3.sub: Every run recorded; every diff searchable.

solutions.title: Ten shapes the work takes
solutions.sub: The six paths above end up in one (or more) of these. Each card is a real, recognizable way to use Fibe — sorted on a quiet little dial from boring-but-essential to brand-new.

production-deployment.title: Production deployment
production-deployment.desc: Run live workloads with zero-downtime updates and a quiet pager.

staging-uat.title: Staging & UAT
staging-uat.desc: Isolated copies of production where QA and acceptance can break things safely.

build-test-ci.title: Build & test in CI
build-test-ci.desc: Full-stack environments wired into your pipeline — not green-box theatre.

internal-tools.title: Internal tools
internal-tools.desc: Dashboards, admin panels, the back-office stuff your team uses every day.

software-distribution.title: Software distribution
software-distribution.desc: Hand your customers a ready-to-run version of your product — without telling them about Docker.

self-hosted-stacks.title: Self-hosted stacks
self-hosted-stacks.desc: Run the open-source equivalents of your SaaS sprawl on your own hosts. Pay for compute, not seats.

build-in-public.title: Build in public
build-in-public.desc: A live URL for whatever you're shipping — viewers, customers, future hires watch the progress.

ephemeral-environments.title: Ephemeral environments
ephemeral-environments.desc: An environment per branch or PR. Spins up on push, lives as long as it's useful, evaporates after.

vibe-coding-on-steroids.title: Vibe coding on steroids
vibe-coding-on-steroids.desc: Instant cloud playgrounds, an AI assistant in the panel, a URL to show off — your idea, alive in ninety seconds.

capture.title: Capture this, don't let it slip
capture.sub:
The boring fundamentals people miss in marketing copy — and the ones that decide
            whether you'd actually run Fibe long-term.

total-control.title: Total control
total-control.list_0: Managed service — sign in, ship, never think about ops.
total-control.list_1: Or self-host the whole thing — laptop, home server, local GPU. Even Fibe never sees your code or your models.
total-control.list_2: Or mix — our web app talking to your hardware, or vice-versa. Any combination.
total-control.kicker: No Google in the loop. No Microsoft. The only third party is Fibe — and even that's opt-in.

flat-pricing.title: Flat fee per server
flat-pricing.list_0: One number per server — pick the box, pay the box. Whatever size, one flat fee.
flat-pricing.list_1: No per-seat anything — invite 5 teammates or 500. Same bill.
flat-pricing.list_2: Outgrew a tier? — just upgrade the server. New tier, same shape: one number.
flat-pricing.kicker: Your bill scales with hardware, not headcount. Predictable on day one, predictable in year three.

boring-core.title: Boring core, AI on top
boring-core.list_0: The product is environments — real, full-stack, isolated Docker hosts. Click a button or spin them programmatically.
boring-core.list_1: Same surface everywhere — web UI, CLI, script, agent. All hitting the same APIs.
boring-core.list_2: AI is a feature, not the point — wire it in everywhere, or never. The product still ships.
boring-core.kicker: The boring part is what you actually use. The AI is what makes it five times faster.

local-parity.title: Run it locally first
local-parity.list_0: Plain docker-compose.yml — Fibe is a thin wrapper, not a custom DSL. Your repo is the source of truth.
local-parity.list_1: Verify CI/CD on your laptop — run the whole pipeline locally. Only push to production once it's green.
local-parity.list_2: Same compose, anywhere — your laptop, Fibe, a teammate's workstation, an air-gapped customer site. Identical behaviour.
local-parity.kicker: Standard Docker. Standard Compose. No magic between them and you.

close.title:
Stop reading.
Start shipping.
close.sub:
Whichever path you picked, the next step is the same — one account, one workshop.
            Spin up your first environment in under a minute.

total-control.title: Total control
total-control.list_0: Managed service — sign in, ship, never think about ops.
total-control.list_1: Or self-host the whole thing — laptop, home server, local GPU. Even Fibe never sees your code or your models.
total-control.list_2: Or mix — our web app talking to your hardware, or vice-versa. Any combination.
total-control.kicker: No Google in the loop. No Microsoft. The only third party is Fibe — and even that's opt-in.

flat-pricing.title: Flat fee per server
flat-pricing.list_0: One number per server — pick the box, pay the box. Whatever size, one flat fee.
flat-pricing.list_1: No per-seat anything — invite 5 teammates or 500. Same bill.
flat-pricing.list_2: Outgrew a tier? — just upgrade the server. New tier, same shape: one number.
flat-pricing.kicker: Your bill scales with hardware, not headcount. Predictable on day one, predictable in year three.

boring-core.title: Boring core, AI on top
boring-core.list_0: The product is environments — real, full-stack, isolated Docker hosts. Click a button or spin them programmatically.
boring-core.list_1: Same surface everywhere — web UI, CLI, script, agent. All hitting the same APIs.
boring-core.list_2: AI is a feature, not the point — wire it in everywhere, or never. The product still ships.
boring-core.kicker: The boring part is what you actually use. The AI is what makes it five times faster.

local-parity.title: Run it locally first
local-parity.list_0: Plain docker-compose.yml — Fibe is a thin wrapper, not a custom DSL. Your repo is the source of truth.
local-parity.list_1: Verify CI/CD on your laptop — run the whole pipeline locally. Only push to production once it's green.
local-parity.list_2: Same compose, anywhere — your laptop, Fibe, a teammate's workstation, an air-gapped customer site. Identical behaviour.
local-parity.kicker: Standard Docker. Standard Compose. No magic between them and you.

close.title:
Stop reading.
Start shipping.
close.sub:
Whichever path you picked, the next step is the same — one account, one workshop.
            Spin up your first environment in under a minute.
